summary: Specifies a family of pure and hybrid check character systems that protect strings against human copying and keying errors, including MOD 97-10 for numeric strings. Under MOD 97-10, letters are mapped to two-digit values (A=10 .. Z=35), the string is read as a base-10 integer, and a valid string satisfies integer mod 97 == 1; this is the check computation behind IBAN check digits.
title: Information technology — Security techniques — Check character systems
